attachment.snippet:
1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + (optionally) rebase /
2	//! fast-forward a workspace's repos against their remotes.
3	//!
4	//! Default is `--check` (fetch + report, error if any repo needs
5	//! action); `--no-check` applies. On any failure every repo is
6	//! reverted to its pre-sync op via `jj op restore`.
7	//!
8	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses
9	//! the same snapshot-and-restore pattern.
10	
11	use std::io::IsTerminal;
12	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
13	use std::process::Command;
14	
15	use clap::Args;
16	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, prompt, run, scope_to_repos};
19	use crate::context::Context;
20	use crate::desc_helpers::VC_CONFIG_FILE;
21	use crate::options_flags::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
22	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
23	use crate::toml_simple;
24	
25	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
26	///
27	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
28	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
29	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
30	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
31	///
32	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
33	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
34	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
35	///
36	/// Repo set is resolved from `-R/--repo` + `--scope`:
37	///
38	/// - `-R PATH` — workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone.
39	/// - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — keyword role selection,
40	///   resolved via the workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
41	/// - Neither — workspace-default scope:
42	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
43	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`
44	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd
45	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
46	pub struct SyncArgs {
47	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
48	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
49	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
50	    pub check: bool,
51	
52	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
53	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
54	    pub no_check: bool,
55	
56	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
57	    #[arg(short, long)]
58	    pub quiet: bool,
59	
60	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
61	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
62	    pub bookmark: String,
63	
64	    /// Remote to sync against
65	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
66	    pub remote: String,
67	
68	    /// Rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced bookmark without asking.
69	    ///
70	    /// After a successful `--no-check` sync, `@` is repositioned onto
71	    /// the synced bookmark. When the code repo's `@` carries changes
72	    /// it normally asks before rebasing; `--rebase` answers yes up
73	    /// front for non-interactive use. Ignored in `--check` mode and
74	    /// for the session repo (which always `jj new main`).
75	    #[arg(long)]
76	    pub rebase: bool,
77	
78	    /// Workspace root, or a single jj repo to sync on its own.
79	    ///
80	    /// - `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at PATH.
81	    /// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — use PATH as the workspace root and
82	    ///   sync the named side(s).
83	    #[arg(short = 'R', long = "repo", value_name = "PATH", verbatim_doc_comment)]
84	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
85	
86	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
87	    ///
88	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`:
89	    ///
90	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
91	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
92	    ///   is configured).
93	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
94	    ///
95	    /// Composes with `-R` as the workspace root. Default depends
96	    /// on workspace state: dual workspace → `code,bot`;
97	    /// single-repo workspace or POR → `code`.
98	    #[arg(
99	        short = 's',
100	        long,
101	        value_name = "SCOPE",
102	        value_parser = parse_scope,
103	        verbatim_doc_comment
104	    )]
105	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
106	}
107	
108	/// Inputs to the sync op, flat, owned, clap-free.
109	///
110	/// - `quiet`: `-q` / `--quiet` — clamp output to `Warn` for the run.
111	/// - `bookmark`: bookmark to sync in each repo (default `main`).
112	/// - `remote`: remote to sync against (default `origin`).
113	/// - `no_check`: `--no-check` — actually rebase / fast-forward
114	///   (absent ⇒ check mode: fetch + report only). The `--check`
115	///   flag is the explicit form of the default and carries no
116	///   value of its own, so it isn't mirrored here.
117	/// - `rebase`: `--rebase` — rebase a non-empty `@` onto the synced
118	///   bookmark without prompting (code repo only; see
119	///   `reposition_code`).
120	/// - `repo`: `-R/--repo` path (None ⇒ discover the workspace
121	///   root from cwd).
122	/// - `scope`: `--scope` parsed (None ⇒ resolve via the
123	///   workspace-default scope at run time).
124	pub struct SyncParams {
125	    pub quiet: bool,
126	    pub bookmark: String,
127	    pub remote: String,
128	    pub no_check: bool,
129	    pub rebase: bool,
130	    pub repo: Option<PathBuf>,
131	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
132	}
133	
134	impl From<&SyncArgs> for SyncParams {
135	    /// Convert clap-derived `SyncArgs` into the flat `SyncParams`.
136	    /// `--check` is dropped — it's the explicit form of the
137	    /// default; the op only ever consults `no_check`.
138	    fn from(a: &SyncArgs) -> Self {
139	        Self {
140	            quiet: a.quiet,
141	            bookmark: a.bookmark.clone(),
142	            remote: a.remote.clone(),
143	            no_check: a.no_check,
144	            rebase: a.rebase,
145	            repo: a.repo.clone(),
146	            scope: a.scope.clone(),
147	        }
148	    }
149	}
150	
151	impl SubcommandRunner for SyncArgs {
152	    type Params = SyncParams;
153	
154	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&SyncArgs>` impl above
155	    /// (total — never fails).
156	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
157	        Ok(SyncParams::from(self))
158	    }
159	
160	    /// Run the existing `sync` op.
161	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
162	        sync(ctx, params)
163	    }
164	}
165	
166	/// Resolve `params` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos`
167	/// operates on.
168	///
169	/// `-R` and `--scope` compose:
170	///
171	/// - neither — workspace-default scope against the discovered root.
172	/// - `-R PATH` alone — sync just that repo.
173	/// - `-s ROLES` alone — roles against the discovered workspace root.
174	/// - `-R PATH -s ROLES` — roles against `PATH` as the workspace root.
175	fn resolve_params_to_repos(
176	    params: &SyncParams,
177	) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
178	    match (&params.repo, &params.scope) {
179	        (None, None) => {
180	            let root = find_workspace_root();
181	            scope_to_repos(&default_scope(root.as_deref()), root.as_deref())
182	        }
183	        (Some(p), None) => Ok(vec![p.clone()]),
184	        (None, Some(s)) => scope_to_repos(s, find_workspace_root().as_deref()),
185	        (Some(p), Some(s)) => scope_to_repos(s, Some(p)),
186	    }
187	}
188	
189	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
190	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
191	pub enum State {
192	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
193	    UpToDate,
194	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
195	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
196	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
197	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
198	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
199	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
200	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
201	    NoRemote,
202	}
203

1	//! Integration tests for the sync module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `sync_repos` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures. Each test builds an isolated fixture (bare-git
5	//! remotes + colocated jj repos) under a unique tempdir via
6	//! `Fixture::new`, then drives the scenario with plain `jj`
7	//! subprocess calls. Requires `jj` in `PATH`.
8	//!
9	//! Fixtures clean themselves up via `Fixture`'s `Drop` impl so a
10	//! panicking test still removes its tempdir.
11	
12	use super::*;
13	use crate::options_flags::scope::Side;
14	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
15	use std::fs;
16	use std::process::Command;
17	
18	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root_from, scope_to_repos};
19	
20	/// Resolver helpers must consume what `init --repo-local` produces.
21	///
22	/// Builds a real dual-repo fixture (bare git remotes + colocated jj
23	/// repos + the canonical `.vc-config.toml` pair init writes), then
24	/// drives the resolver chain `sync()` uses when `-R` is empty:
25	///
26	/// - `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`
27	///   (proves cwd-portability against init's `path = "/.claude"` /
28	///   `path = "/"` config split).
29	/// - `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` reads the workspace config and
30	///   resolves to the dual-repo default.
31	/// - `scope_to_repos` maps each `Scope` shape to the right
32	///   absolute path(s) under the fixture.
33	///
34	/// Pure check on the resolver chain — does not invoke `sync()`
35	/// itself, since that walks `std::env::current_dir()` and parallel
36	/// `cargo test` makes cwd mutation unsafe.
37	#[test]
38	fn resolver_chain_against_init_repo_local() {
39	    let fx = Fixture::new("resolver-chain");
40	
41	    // Walk-up from the bot side lands on the app root.
42	    assert_eq!(
43	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude).as_deref(),
44	        Some(&*fx.work),
45	        "find_workspace_root should resolve from .claude up to work"
46	    );
47	    // Walk-up from the app root finds itself.
48	    assert_eq!(
49	        find_workspace_root_from(&fx.work).as_deref(),
50	        Some(&*fx.work)
51	    );
52	
53	    // init --repo-local writes other-repo = ".claude", so the
54	    // workspace's default scope is dual.
55	    assert_eq!(
56	        default_scope(Some(&fx.work)),
57	        Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot])
58	    );
59	
60	    // Each scope shape resolves to the right absolute path(s).
61	    assert_eq!(
62	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
63	        vec![fx.work.clone(), fx.claude.clone()]
64	    );
65	    assert_eq!(
66	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
67	        vec![fx.work.clone()]
68	    );
69	    assert_eq!(
70	        scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap(),
71	        vec![fx.claude.clone()]
72	    );
73	
74	    // sync_repos accepts the resolved list and reports up-to-date
75	    // — the resolver's output is shaped the way sync expects.
76	    let resolved = scope_to_repos(&Scope(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]), Some(&fx.work)).unwrap();
77	    sync_repos(&resolved, &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed on resolved repos");
78	}
79	
80	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and assert success; returns trimmed stdout.
81	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
82	    let out = Command::new("jj")
83	        .args(args)
84	        .arg("-R")
85	        .arg(repo)
86	        .output()
87	        .expect("spawn jj");
88	    assert!(
89	        out.status.success(),
90	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
91	        repo.display(),
92	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
93	    );
94	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
95	}
96	
97	/// Resolve `rev` to its short commit id in `repo`.
98	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
99	    jj(
100	        repo,
101	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
102	    )
103	}
104	
105	/// Sync params with `--no-check` set (apply mode).
106	///
107	/// Integration tests pass explicit repo paths through `sync_repos`
108	/// directly, so `repo` / `scope` stay `None` here and the CLI-side
109	/// default resolution is not exercised by this helper.
110	fn apply_params() -> SyncParams {
111	    SyncParams {
112	        no_check: true,
113	        quiet: false,
114	        bookmark: "main".to_string(),
115	        remote: "origin".to_string(),
116	        rebase: false,
117	        repo: None,
118	        scope: None,
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	/// Apply-mode params with `--rebase` set (auto-confirm the code-repo
123	/// non-empty `@` rebase).
124	fn rebase_params() -> SyncParams {
125	    SyncParams {
126	        rebase: true,
127	        ..apply_params()
128	    }
129	}
130	
131	/// True when `revset` matches at least one commit in `repo`.
132	fn has(repo: &Path, revset: &str) -> bool {
133	    !jj(
134	        repo,
135	        &[
136	            "log",
137	            "-r",
138	            revset,
139	            "--no-graph",
140	            "-T",
141	            r#"commit_id.short(12) ++ "\n""#,
142	        ],
143	    )
144	    .trim()
145	    .is_empty()
146	}
147	
148	/// Add a local-only commit on `main` in `repo` (not pushed), then
149	/// restore `@` to an empty child so pre-flight still passes.
150	///
151	/// Sequence: write file → describe `@` → advance `main` to `@` →
152	/// create a fresh empty `@` above it.
153	fn add_local_commit(repo: &Path, file: &str, content: &str, msg: &str) {
154	    fs::write(repo.join(file), content).expect("write local file");
155	    jj(repo, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
156	    jj(repo, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
157	    jj(repo, &["new"]);
158	}
159	
160	/// Clone `remote_url` into `<base>/<work_name>`, add a commit, push it.
161	///
162	/// Used to make the remote advance beyond the fixture's `main`
163	/// from a separate working copy. Returns the pushed commit's id.
164	fn push_from_clone(
165	    base: &Path,
166	    remote_url: &Path,
167	    work_name: &str,
168	    file: &str,
169	    content: &str,
170	    msg: &str,
171	) -> String {
172	    let workdir = base.join(work_name);
173	    let out = Command::new("jj")
174	        .args(["git", "clone", "--colocate"])
175	        .arg(remote_url)
176	        .arg(&workdir)
177	        .output()
178	        .expect("spawn jj clone");
179	    assert!(
180	        out.status.success(),
181	        "jj git clone failed: {}",
182	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
183	    );
184	    fs::write(workdir.join(file), content).expect("write remote file");
185	    jj(&workdir, &["describe", "@", "-m", msg]);
186	    jj(&workdir, &["bookmark", "set", "main", "-r", "@"]);
187	    jj(&workdir, &["git", "push", "--bookmark", "main"]);
188	    cid(&workdir, "main")
189	}
190	
191	/// Scenario 1: fresh fixture, nothing to do — `sync` leaves both
192	/// repos untouched.
193	#[test]
194	fn sync_up_to_date() {
195	    let fx = Fixture::new("up-to-date");
196	    let work_main = cid(&fx.work, "main");
197	    let claude_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
198	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
199	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.work, "main"), work_main);
200	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), claude_main);
201	}
202	
203	/// Scenario 2a: a non-empty `@` on top of main (simulates `/exit`
204	/// trailing session writes in `.claude`) when there's nothing new on
205	/// the remote. The session repo always `jj new main`s: `@` becomes a
206	/// fresh empty child of the unmoved main, and the trailing commit is
207	/// preserved as a non-empty sibling head (no longer in the working
208	/// copy).
209	#[test]
210	fn sync_session_jj_new_when_up_to_date() {
211	    let fx = Fixture::new("session-jjnew-uptodate");
212	    let pre_main = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
213	    fs::write(fx.claude.join("trailing.jsonl"), "{\"line\":1}\n").expect("write trailing file");
214	    sync_repos(&fx.repos(), &apply_params()).expect("sync should succeed");
215	    // main didn't move.
216	    assert_eq!(cid(&fx.claude, "main"), pre_main, "main should not move");
217	    // @ is a fresh empty child of main.

content.text:
`0.66.0-1` implementation is complete and the full cargo cycle passes (fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, 382+ tests, install). Stopping for Work review before writing the commit description.

**What changed (4 files):**

- **`src/sync.rs`** — the reposition feature:
  - Added `--rebase` bool to `SyncArgs` / `SyncParams` (+ `From` wiring, doc comments).
  - Moved `@`-repositioning out of `run_plan` into `sync_repos`, running *after* `run_plan` succeeds and **outside** the `op_restore` revert region (apply-mode only) — a reposition failure no longer rolls back the successful fetch/fast-forward.
  - Removed `ensure_at_on_main` (the old rebase-@ step); replaced with `reposition_at` dispatcher + `reposition_code`, `reposition_session`, plus helpers `is_session_repo` (reads `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace] path`), `confirm_rebase` (TTY-gated prompt; non-interactive → skip), `at_is_empty`.
  - Rules exactly as speced: code repo → `jj new xxx` when `xxx` is a proper descendant of `@-` and `@` empty; non-empty → rebase gated by `--rebase`/prompt; `xxx == @-` no-op; not-a-descendant → leave + inform. Session repo → `jj new main` when `@-` on main, else error.

- **`src/sync/integration_tests.rs`** — rewrote the two `.claude` trailing-`@` tests to the new `jj new` semantics (trailing commit preserved as a sibling head, `@` moved off it), and added 4 tests: session off-main error, code fast-forward `jj new`, code skip-without-`--rebase`, code rebase-with-`--rebase`. Added `rebase_params` + `has` helpers.

- **`src/sync/tests.rs`** — `parse_rebase_flag` test + default `rebase == false` assertion.

- **`notes/todo.md`** — ladder marker `0.66.0-1 … (done)`.

Ready to commit. Please review the diff (`jj diff -R .`) — particularly the session-repo behavior change (trailing session `@` now becomes a sibling head rather than being carried forward), since that's the observable semantic shift from the old `ensure_at_on_main`.

Here's the proposed commit description for `0.66.0-1`:

**Title:**
```
feat: reposition @ after sync (0.66.0-1)
```

**Body:**
```
After a successful apply-mode sync, reposition @ onto the
freshly-synced bookmark, replacing the old ensure_at_on_main
rebase step.

- src/sync.rs
  - add --rebase flag to SyncArgs / SyncParams (+ From wiring)
  - move @-reposition out of run_plan into sync_repos, run
    after success and outside the op_restore revert region
    (apply mode only)
  - remove ensure_at_on_main; add reposition_at dispatcher
    with reposition_code / reposition_session
  - add helpers is_session_repo (.vc-config.toml path),
    confirm_rebase (TTY-gated prompt), at_is_empty
- src/sync/integration_tests.rs
  - rewrite the two session trailing-@ tests to the jj-new
    semantics (trailing commit kept as a sibling head)
  - add session off-main error + code jj-new / skip-rebase /
    rebase-with-flag tests; add rebase_params / has helpers
- src/sync/tests.rs
  - add parse_rebase_flag; assert default rebase == false
- notes/todo.md
  - mark the 0.66.0-1 ladder rung done
```

May I commit and push this rung to `main` (keep-separate, via `vc-x1 push`, as chosen for this cycle)? As before, once I invoke the push it's a hard stop and I'll wait for you.

file.content:
## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0)**

After `vc-x1 sync --no-check` fast-forwards a bookmark to the
updated remote, `@` is often left parented on the pre-fetch
tip. Add a final apply-mode pass that moves `@` onto the
freshly-synced bookmark, per-repo, only when it's safe —
folding in / replacing the current `ensure_at_on_main` rebase.

Rules (code repo: `xxx` = the synced `--bookmark`; `@-` =
parent of `@`):

- `xxx` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` empty → `jj new xxx`.
- `xxx` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` non-empty → rebase
  `@` onto `xxx`, gated by `--rebase` (else prompt / skip +
  inform).
- `xxx == @-` → already positioned, no-op.
- `xxx` not a descendant of `@-` → leave `@`, inform why.
- `.claude`: `@-` on `main` (ancestor-or-equal) →
  `jj new main`, else error.
- Reposition failures don't roll back the successful fetch /
  fast-forward — the pass sits outside the `op_restore` revert
  region.

Plan ladder:

- 0.66.0-0 Preparation (done) — backfill 0.65.2 Commits
  ref, bump to 0.66.0-0, pick up this entry, open the chores
  section.
- 0.66.0-1 reposition pass (done) — `--rebase` flag +
  helpers (session-repo id via `.vc-config.toml` path, `@` /
  `@-` empty and ancestry probes) + the code and `.claude`
  decision rules, wired as a final apply-mode pass in
  `sync_repos` outside the `op_restore` revert region; fold
  out `ensure_at_on_main`; unit + integration tests. (Scaffold
  and pass are one commit — an unconsumed flag / helper trips
  `-D warnings`.)
- 0.66.0 close-out — Done entry, move `## In Progress` →
  chores, README update for `--rebase`.

## Todo

433	### sync
434	
435	Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes in a single command.
436	Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
437	`.claude`); narrow it with `-s` / `--scope`, or point at a different
438	workspace root or single repo with `-R` / `--repo`. Check-only by
439	default — re-run with `--no-check` to apply.
440	
441	Per repo, `sync` classifies the local bookmark against its remote:
442	
443	| State | Meaning | Action on `--no-check` |
444	|------|---------|--------------------------|
445	| up-to-date | local == remote | none |
446	| behind | local is ancestor of remote | `jj bookmark set <b> -r <b>@<remote>` |
447	| ahead | remote is ancestor of local | none (push is a separate step) |
448	| diverged | neither is ancestor | `jj rebase -b <local-head> -d <b>@<remote>` |
449	| no remote | bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart | none — skip |
450	
451	After the bookmark action above, `sync` also rebases `@` onto the
452	(possibly advanced) bookmark when `@` isn't already a descendant —
453	without this step, `jj git fetch`'s auto-fast-forward would leave `@`
454	dangling off the pre-fetch bookmark commit. This matters for `.claude`,
455	where `/exit`'s trailing session writes always sit on `@`.
456	
457	On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
458	restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
459	every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
460	across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
461	content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
462	rebase are abandoned on the way back.
463	
464	```
465	vc-x1 sync                            # workspace-default scope, --check
466	vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
467	vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # workspace-default scope, apply
468	vc-x1 sync --scope=code               # only the app repo
469	vc-x1 sync --scope=bot                # only the bot repo
470	vc-x1 sync --scope=code,bot           # both (explicit form of the dual default)
471	vc-x1 sync -R ../other                # sync ../other as a single repo
472	vc-x1 sync -R ../other --scope=code,bot   # ../other as workspace root
473	```
474	
475	Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
476	explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
477	explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
478	default.
479	
480	**Repo set resolution.** `-R` and `--scope` compose:
481	
482	1. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
483	   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
484	   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
485	2. `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at `PATH`.
486	3. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` alone — workspace roles, resolved
487	   via the discovered workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`
488	   (`code` → root, `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
489	4. `-R PATH --scope=ROLES` — roles resolved against `PATH` as the
490	   workspace root.
491	
492	Scope is cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to
493	the workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.
494	
495	| Flag | Description |
496	|------|-------------|
497	| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone. Composes with `--scope` |
498	| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Composes with `-R` |
499	| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
500	| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
501	| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
502	| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
503	| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |
504	
505	**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:
506	
507	- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
508	  `sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`. Nothing else.
509	  Makes "sprinkle sync everywhere" genuinely cheap. Scope is
510	  bookmark-vs-remote tracking — `@` may have uncommitted
511	  working-copy changes; sync intentionally doesn't speak to
512	  that (use `jj st` for working-copy state).
513	- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
514	  state lines, then **fatal in `--check` mode** with
515	  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
516	  and re-run`. Under `--no-check`, the actions run instead.
517	- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
518	  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.
519	
520	**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
521	tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
522	remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
523	`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
524	configs where auto-advance is disabled.

file.content:
### sync

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes in a single command.
Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
`.claude`); narrow it with `-s` / `--scope`, or point at a different
workspace root or single repo with `-R` / `--repo`. Check-only by
default — re-run with `--no-check` to apply.

Per repo, `sync` classifies the local bookmark against its remote:

| State | Meaning | Action on `--no-check` |
|------|---------|--------------------------|
| up-to-date | local == remote | none |
| behind | local is ancestor of remote | `jj bookmark set <b> -r <b>@<remote>` |
| ahead | remote is ancestor of local | none (push is a separate step) |
| diverged | neither is ancestor | `jj rebase -b <local-head> -d <b>@<remote>` |
| no remote | bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart | none — skip |

After the bookmark action above, `sync` also rebases `@` onto the
(possibly advanced) bookmark when `@` isn't already a descendant —
without this step, `jj git fetch`'s auto-fast-forward would leave `@`
dangling off the pre-fetch bookmark commit. This matters for `.claude`,
where `/exit`'s trailing session writes always sit on `@`.

On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
rebase are abandoned on the way back.

```
vc-x1 sync                            # workspace-default scope, --check
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # workspace-default scope, apply
vc-x1 sync --scope=code               # only the app repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=bot                # only the bot repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=code,bot           # both (explicit form of the dual default)
vc-x1 sync -R ../other                # sync ../other as a single repo
vc-x1 sync -R ../other --scope=code,bot   # ../other as workspace root
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

**Repo set resolution.** `-R` and `--scope` compose:

1. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
2. `-R PATH` alone — sync just the repo at `PATH`.
3. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` alone — workspace roles, resolved
   via the discovered workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`
   (`code` → root, `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
4. `-R PATH --scope=ROLES` — roles resolved against `PATH` as the
   workspace root.

Scope is cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to
the workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Workspace root, or a single repo to sync alone. Composes with `--scope` |
| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Composes with `-R` |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
  `sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`. Nothing else.
  Makes "sprinkle sync everywhere" genuinely cheap. Scope is
  bookmark-vs-remote tracking — `@` may have uncommitted
  working-copy changes; sync intentionally doesn't speak to
  that (use `jj st` for working-copy state).
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
  state lines, then **fatal in `--check` mode** with
  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
  and re-run`. Under `--no-check`, the actions run instead.
- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.

**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
configs where auto-advance is disabled.

content.text: Now update the README. First, replace the `@`-reposition paragraph with the new per-repo behavior:

input.old_string:
After the bookmark action above, `sync` also rebases `@` onto the
(possibly advanced) bookmark when `@` isn't already a descendant —
without this step, `jj git fetch`'s auto-fast-forward would leave `@`
dangling off the pre-fetch bookmark commit. This matters for `.claude`,
where `/exit`'s trailing session writes always sit on `@`.

On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
rebase are abandoned on the way back.
input.new_string:
After the bookmark action above, and only under `--no-check`, `sync`
repositions `@` onto the freshly-synced bookmark as a final pass — run
after every repo syncs cleanly, and *outside* the failure-revert
region below, so a reposition problem never rolls back a good
fetch/fast-forward. The rule differs by repo (`@-` is the parent of
`@`; `<b>` is the synced `--bookmark`):

- **Code repo** (the app / workspace root):
  - `@` is clean (empty) and `<b>` sits ahead of `@-` on the same
    line → `jj new <b>` starts a fresh `@` on the new tip (the old
    empty `@` is auto-abandoned).
  - `@` has changes → `sync` asks before moving it; pass `--rebase`
    to answer yes up front (`jj rebase -b @ -d <b>`). Declined — or
    not a TTY and no `--rebase` — leaves `@` in place and says so.
  - `@` already sits on `<b>`, or `<b>` isn't on `@-`'s line
    (diverged / `@` ahead) → `@` is left untouched, with a note why.
- **Session repo** (`.claude`): always `jj new main` when `@-` is on
  `main`, so a fresh empty `@` starts each session on the tip; the
  prior `@` (e.g. `/exit`'s trailing session writes) is preserved as
  a sibling head. If `@-` isn't on `main`, `sync` errors rather than
  strand it.

On any failure during fetch/classify/act — conflicted rebase,
subprocess error, anything — `sync` restores every repo to its
starting state via `jj op restore`. Either every repo advances or
none do. Working-copy files are preserved across the revert: jj
rewinds the operation log but leaves disk content untouched, and any
conflicted commits introduced by the failed rebase are abandoned on
the way back. The `@`-reposition pass runs *after* this region, so a
reposition error is surfaced without undoing the successful sync.

input.old_string:
## feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0)

# References
input.new_string:
## feat: reposition @ onto synced bookmark (0.66.0)

After `vc-x1 sync --no-check` fast-forwards a bookmark to the
updated remote, `@` was often left parented on the pre-fetch
tip. `sync` now runs a final apply-mode pass that repositions
`@` onto the freshly-synced bookmark, per-repo, replacing the
old `ensure_at_on_main` rebase step.

Rules (code repo: `xxx` = the synced `--bookmark`; `@-` =
parent of `@`):

- `xxx` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` empty → `jj new xxx`.
- `xxx` a proper descendant of `@-`, `@` non-empty → rebase
  `@` onto `xxx`, gated by `--rebase` (else prompt on a TTY;
  skip + inform when declined or non-interactive).
- `xxx == @-` → already positioned, no-op.
- `xxx` not a descendant of `@-` → leave `@`, inform why.
- `.claude`: `@-` on `main` (ancestor-or-equal) → `jj new
  main`, else error. The prior `@` (session writes) is kept
  as a sibling head.

The pass runs after `run_plan` succeeds and *outside* the
`op_restore` revert region, so a reposition failure (e.g. a
session `@-` off main) is surfaced without rolling back the
successful fetch / fast-forward.

### Design notes

- Repo role is re-derived from each repo's `.vc-config.toml`
  `[workspace] path` (`/` = code, `/.claude` = session), since
  the `Vec<PathBuf>` reaching `sync_repos` has dropped the
  `Code`/`Bot` role.
- Scaffold and pass landed as one commit: an unconsumed
  `--rebase` field or helper trips `clippy -D warnings`
  (unused field / dead_code), so the flag and its consumer
  don't split cleanly.
- The non-empty-`@` rebase prompt is TTY-gated: no terminal
  and no `--rebase` → skip + inform rather than block on
  `read_line`.

### As-built ladder

Keep-separate shape — each rung pushed as its own commit on
`main` via `vc-x1 push`:

- 0.66.0-0 Preparation: backfill 0.65.2 Commits ref, bump
  version, pick up the entry, open this chores section.
- 0.66.0-1 reposition pass: `--rebase` flag; `reposition_at`
  dispatcher with `reposition_code` / `reposition_session`;
  `is_session_repo` / `confirm_rebase` / `at_is_empty`
  helpers; `ensure_at_on_main` removed; unit + integration
  tests.
- 0.66.0 close-out: this bookkeeping commit + README docs and
  examples.

### Outcome

`vc-x1 sync --no-check` leaves `@` freshly seated on the synced
bookmark: a clean code-repo `@` via `jj new`, a dirty one
rebased only with `--rebase` (or an interactive yes), and the
`.claude` journal restarted on `main` each sync. Documented in
the README `### sync` section.

# References

190	        Per repo:\n  \
191	          - up-to-date        nothing to do\n  \
192	          - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote\n  \
193	          - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)\n  \
194	          - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts\n  \
